originalTitle: Wall Street ends lower as tech shares slip
summary: STORY: U.S. stocks finished flat to slightly lower on Wednesday, with the Dow little changed, the S&P 500 down more than two-tenths of a percent and the Nasdaq sliding two-thirds of a percent. Speaking at a European Central Bank panel in Portugal, U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said he will stick firmly to the central bank's 2% inflation target and "disappoint" anyone who expects loose monetary policy despite President Donald Trump's call for interest rate cuts. But Brad Bernstein, managing director at UBS Private Wealth Management, doesn't think the Fed will hike rates this year either. "One of the three reasons we think markets will be higher at the end of the year is because the Fed is not going to raise interest rates this year. // We think more likely they'll resume Fed cuts in the spring of next year. And today, Fed Chair Walsh said in Portugal that he has seen in the last four weeks expectations, inflation expectations continuing to get better because of the drop most likely he - didn't cite this - but most likely because oil has gone from 90 [dollars a barrel] to 70 and now under 70 today." Among Wednesday's stock moves, technology shares fell, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index down more than 6%. One tech outlier was Meta Platforms after Bloomberg News reported that it is building a cloud business to sell excess AI computing capacity. Among the day's decliners, shares of Alcoa fell about 9% after Australia's South32 agreed to sell most of its aluminum assets to Alcoa. The key monthly U.S. jobs report is due out on Thursday, while the market will be closed Friday ahead of the Fourth of July holiday.

But Brad Bernstein, managing director at UBS Private Wealth Management, doesn't think the Fed will hike rates this year either. "One of the three reasons we think markets will be higher at the end of the year is because the Fed is not going to raise interest rates this year. // We think more likely they'll resume Fed cuts in the spring of next year. And today, Fed Chair Walsh said in Portugal that he has seen in the last four weeks expectations, inflation expectations continuing to get better because of the drop most likely he - didn't cite this - but most likely because oil has gone from 90 [dollars a barrel] to 70 and now under 70 today." Among Wednesday's stock moves, technology shares fell, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index down more than 6%. chineseTitle: 科技股下滑，华尔街收低
chineseSummary: 故事：美国股市周三收盘持平或小幅下跌，道琼斯指数几乎没有变化，标准普尔 500 指数下跌超过十分之二，纳斯达克指数下跌三分之二。美联储主席凯文·沃什在葡萄牙举行的欧洲央行小组会议上发表讲话时表示，尽管美国总统特朗普呼吁降息，但他将坚定地坚持央行 2% 的通胀目标，这让任何预计宽松货币政策的人“失望”。但瑞银私人财富管理公司董事总经理布拉德·伯恩斯坦也不认为美联储今年会加息。 “我们认为年底市场走高的三个原因之一是美联储今年不会加息。//我们认为他们更有可能在明年春季恢复美联储降息。今天，美联储主席沃尔什在葡萄牙表示，他在过去四个星期内看到了通胀预期继续好转的原因，他很可能没有提到这一点，但很可能是因为油价已从 90 美元/桶跌至 70 美元，现在低于每桶 70 美元。今天70了。”周三股市走势中，科技股下跌，费城半导体指数跌幅超过 6%。彭博新闻社报道 Meta Platforms 正在建立一项云业务以出售多余的人工智能计算能力后，其中一个科技异常现象就是 Meta Platforms。在当天的跌幅中，美国铝业公司 (Alcoa) 股价下跌约 9%，此前澳大利亚 South32 同意将其大部分铝资产出售给美国铝业公司 (Alcoa)。美国重要的月度就业报告将于周四发布，而周五市场将在 7 月 4 日假期之前休市。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
